title: Chapter Seventy-Nine: Disarmed
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The moment he broke the sword energy with Advancing Thunder's fist wave and one Radiant Wing Step, he recognised what was happening.

*Returning to basics.*

Mastery. The verses being engraved in his whole body's meridians. He felt it the way you feel warmth from a fire you cannot see.

Hwa-sin's face entered his vision. Gritted teeth, but his complexion was calm -- the composure of a man who could overwhelm with force even when pushed back in technique.

*Now. Decide it here. True qi running out. Dragged longer -- I lose.*

He thrust the Desolate Sword with his right hand. Simultaneously, his left hand raised opposing formulas. Mind clear. Power deployed from the Inner Gate point below his wrist. Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm wrapping blood energy that spread like wildfire.

Eternal Blossom Fist's incomplete fourth move.

White sword light filled his vision. Hwa-sin had deflected Radiant Sword Style and was already countering -- Azure Sky Boundless Sword rushing in like invisible arrows, the air splitting and howling.

Yeon-shin spread his palm without flinching.

Hwa-sin's form whirled. Unhindered Clear Flow Body -- exquisite, encompassing evasion and counterattack in a single curved line. He would slash from the side. Land the killing blow.

*As expected.*

Yeon-shin had known. White Qilin was wary of the fourth move's power. He had used it once already, and Hwa-sin did not know its flaw.

*That's enough.*

He bent his hand backwards. Not targeting the front.

Palm force radiating behind him. He filled the Pericardium Channel with overlapping true qi of the Fate Defying Scripture, then burst the opposing methods simultaneously.

BANG.

It was like a bomb detonated in his arm. His vision distorted. Tremendous load fell on his left foot. He spun -- one full rotation, enduring with internal energy, the world blurring into streaks of light and shadow.

Hwa-sin's eyes were wide at the edge of his vision. Having already changed direction once for his movement technique, even White Qilin could not respond again at this distance.

The speed of Unhindered Clear Flow Body had carried Hwa-sin straight into the trap.

SWOOSH.

One whole-body rotation. Truly like a gale.

Innate sense shone. The uncontrollable whirlpool of force guided his body -- ankle to thigh to waist to arm in an instant.

He squeezed his fibularis muscle. Twisted the flow of power upward. Reached his right arm.

CLANG.

One sword went flying.

The spinning blade flashed ceaselessly -- catching sunlight, catching shadow -- before falling to the ground with a sound that rang across the silent training ground like a funeral bell.

Yeon-shin's body spun two more rounds with the Desolate Sword before coming to a stop. His robe's hem fluttered once and fell. He was gripping his sword. Sunlight rippled long on the blade.

The last move was Charged Strike. The very first Battle technique he had learned from watching Cheong Myeong's sword. He had truly unleashed all his martial arts.

*It hurts.*

His grip ached. Numbing vibrations ran to his wrist.

Silence.

White Qilin Namgung Hwa-sin had lost his sword. The young genius who had mastered the ancient divine techniques of the Eight Families had lost to a younger master.

No one spoke. The words would not come.

Then -- the one who broke the silence was the man himself.

"It's not even real combat," Hwa-sin said slowly. "It would be petty to exchange more moves with fist techniques."

The corners of his mouth rose. No hesitation in the gesture as he raised his hands to clasp them. He had fought with everything he had, and he smiled as if the martial match itself -- apart from any reward -- had been worth the losing.

"Young Hero Jeong. It was a good match. I lost."

*At the entrance exam... my sword flew away.*

The memory surfaced unbidden. Losing his grip. Unable to handle Hwa-sin's sword force. Relying on White Qilin's generosity. That had been just yesterday, and a day like today had come.

*Proof of growth.*

Yeon-shin straightened his posture. True qi spread through stiffened meridians. He raised his hands in return greeting.

"I learned a great deal. I can only be grateful."

They faced each other -- Radiant Demon Wing's Lightning Genius and Obeying Heaven Wing's White Qilin -- and the sight of them paying respects was the sight of Desolate Fortress's future.

The crowd erupted. Admiration and cheers filled the central practice ground.

The Annihilation Team Leader approached. Iron energy waves wrapped his gaunt body. "Lightning Genius will face the Namgung young master," he said. "White Qilin, behead the Flourishing Spirit Flower Master."

Hwa-sin clasped his hands with a stiff face. "I accept the competition's result."

The Annihilation Team Leader turned to Yeon-shin. Something strange in his gaze.

"I saw you refining your martial arts in this place. They are precious techniques -- too valuable to be buried. If you make secret manuals, there will be many who would shed blood to possess those books." His hand gripped Yeon-shin's shoulder. Hard. "Put effort into safeguarding your martial arts."

He walked away. Hwa-sin shrugged and approached.

"Seems like words worth heeding. Extraordinary martial arts tend to call forth blood. Haven't unnamed heavenly demons' graves been dug up countless times?"

"I've heard well."

Hwa-sin's face lightened. The smile returned -- broad, genuine, the kind of smile that belonged on a man who had never needed to hide anything.

"I've suffered consecutive defeats. To one Young Hero."

"Who knows what would happen if we cross swords again."

"For now, true. But do you know? I must be the one who has experienced your growth momentum firsthand. At Desolate City, and now." His gaze sharpened. "About a year from now, your opponents will be different. Even among the Thirteen Evil Sects or Eight Orthodox Families. At least not peers."

Yeon-shin said nothing. It was awkward. But the words were not wrong. That was how it had to be to reach the Divine Sword Squad Leader's rank.

"But now is now." Hwa-sin's expression turned serious. "If I dare advise -- hone those mysterious palm methods and sword verses. And increase internal energy as much as possible. Whatever spirit pills you must use."

He paused.

"My brother is strong as a person himself. His martial arts too. I never won until leaving the main family. Best not to think of winning with power. The spirit medicines my brother consumed as the Namgung family's legitimate son -- he would rank among the top under heaven."

"I'll keep it in mind."

Yeon-shin bowed his head slightly. He had already found the way. What he needed was time.

"The day has grown long," Hwa-sin said.

The sunset burned. Red shadows stretched without signs of dusk falling. People left one by one. The Grand Administrator approached with satisfaction seeping from his gaze.

"Official notice delivery will take time. We must first declare war on the Namgung family. But you should receive results within seven days."

Seven days.

*The full moon is approaching.*

---
